title: Pension Credit — Guarantee Credit
jurisdiction: England, Wales and Scotland (this file assumes you live in Great Britain)

effective.uprating: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2026 (S.I. 2026/148), art. 29

plainly: A weekly top-up to a minimum income for people over State Pension age — the famously unclaimed one, and the key that opens several other entitlements.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to work out whether you have reached State Pension age — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.1(2)(b) (the qualifying age); Pensions Act 1995 Sch 4 para 1 (what that age is)

questions.label: Do you live with a partner (married, civil partner, or as a couple)?
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.17(1) (“couple”); s.5 (a couple's income and capital are assessed together)

questions.label: Your partner's date of birth
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.4(1A) (no entitlement while one of a couple is under the qualifying age)

questions.label: If one of you is under State Pension age: do you get pension-age Housing Benefit as a couple?
questions.help: A small number of couples keep Pension Credit under transitional protection this way. If unsure, answer no.
questions.rule: Welfare Reform Act 2012 (Commencement No. 31 etc.) Order 2019 (S.I. 2019/37), savings provisions; gov.uk Pension Credit eligibility

questions.label: Your weekly State Pension (and your partner's, added together)
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.15(1)(c) and s.16(1) (retirement pension income counts as income)

questions.label: Weekly income from workplace or private pensions and annuities (both of you)
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.16(1)(f)–(i) (occupational and personal pension income counts)

questions.label: Weekly earnings from work, after tax (both of you)
questions.help: Enter 0 if none. A small amount is disregarded — the working-out below shows it.
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.15(1)(a) (earnings count); SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 6 (what is disregarded)

questions.label: Other weekly income that counts — for example Carer's Allowance or Working Tax Credit (both of you)
questions.help: Do NOT include Attendance Allowance, DLA, PIP, Pension Age Disability Payment or Housing Benefit — those are not counted as income here. Enter 0 if none.
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.15(1); SPC Regulations 2002 reg 15(1) (which benefits count as income)

questions.label: Total savings and investments (both of you) — not your home
questions.help: The first £10,000 is ignored entirely; there is no upper limit, but savings above £10,000 count as a little income each week.
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.15(2); SPC Regulations 2002 reg 15(6) (deemed income from capital)

questions.label: Do you get Attendance Allowance, the daily-living part of PIP or ADP, the middle or higher care rate of DLA, or Pension Age Disability Payment?
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(7); SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part I para 1 (severe disability additional amount)

questions.label: Does your partner get one of those benefits too?
questions.rule: SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part I para 1 (a couple's severe-disability amount needs both to qualify)

questions.label: Apart from your partner, do any other adults (18 or over) normally live with you?
questions.rule: SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part I para 1 (the severe-disability amount needs no non-dependants residing)

questions.label: Does anyone get Carer's Allowance (or Scotland's carer support payment) for looking after you or your partner?
questions.rule: SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part I para 1 (no severe-disability amount while a carer is paid for the care)

questions.label: Are you entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment) for looking after someone else?
questions.help: Answer yes even if it is not actually paid because of your State Pension (an “underlying entitlement” still counts).
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(8) (carer additional amount); SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part II para 4

questions.label: Is your partner entitled to Carer's Allowance (or carer support payment)?
questions.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(8)(b); SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 1 Part II para 4

values.plainly: The standard minimum guarantee for your household (2026–27: £238.00 single, £363.25 couple).
values.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(3)(a), (5); SPC Regulations 2002 reg 6(1), as uprated by S.I. 2026/148 art 29

values.plainly: Deemed weekly income from those savings: £1 for each £500 (or part of £500) above £10,000.
Earnings ignored each week: £20 where a carer's amount applies, otherwise £10 for a couple, £5 single.
Earnings that count, after the disregard.
All the income that counts, added up: pensions, counted earnings, other counted income, and the deemed income from savings.
The severe-disability additional amount, where it applies (2026–27: £86.05, or £172.10 for a couple who both qualify).
values.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(7); SPC Regulations 2002 reg 6(4)–(5), Sch 1 Part I para 1

values.plainly: The carer additional amount (2026–27: £48.15 each, where you or your partner are entitled to Carer's Allowance).
values.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.2(8); SPC Regulations 2002 reg 6(6)(a), (8), Sch 1 Part II para 4

values.plainly: Your minimum guarantee: the standard amount plus any additional amounts.
Guarantee Credit: the difference between your minimum guarantee and your counted income.

gates.because: You have not yet reached State Pension age — Pension Credit starts there. (Working-age support is Universal Credit, which this page does not cover.)
gates.rule: State Pension Credit Act 2002 s.1(2)(b); Pensions Act 1995 Sch 4 para 1

gates.because: A couple cannot get Pension Credit while one partner is under State Pension age (unless protected by pension-age Housing Benefit). Until then, support is Universal Credit.

verdicts.because: Your counted income appears to be below your minimum guarantee, so Guarantee Credit would top it up by the weekly amount shown — and being on Pension Credit opens the passported entitlements below.

Your counted income appears to be at or above your minimum guarantee. Close cases are worth claiming anyway — an award of even a few pence opens the passported entitlements, and this page cannot see everything (see what it does not encode).

not_asked: anything about the person your carer's entitlement is for
not_encoded: Savings Credit (closed to most people reaching State Pension age after 5 April 2016; SPC Act 2002 s.3)
housing-costs amounts for service charges and ground rent (SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 2)
the £20 earnings disregards other than carers' (Sch 6 paras 1–2: lone parents, some special occupations)
the severe-disability amount for a couple where only one qualifies (Sch 1 para 1(2): possible in some cases, e.g. where the other is registered blind)
notional and deprived capital (reg 21: capital given away can still be counted)
the Great Britain presence and habitual-residence rules (s.1(2)(a), s.1(5))

passports.name: Free TV licence at 75
passports.note: Pension Credit is the qualifying condition.

passports.name: Winter Fuel Payment
passports.note: linked to Pension Credit in England and Wales; the rules have moved twice in two years — check the current position when you claim.

passports.name: Warm Home Discount
passports.note: Guarantee Credit is the core qualifying group.

passports.name: Free NHS dental treatment, sight tests and help with health costs
Maximum Housing Benefit
passports.note: on Guarantee Credit, income and savings are disregarded and rent is normally covered in full — worked out below.

sources_checked.where: SPC Regulations 2002 reg 6 as in force (legislation.gov.uk revised text) — cross-checked against gov.uk/pension-credit/what-youll-get
SPC Regulations 2002 reg 15(6) (legislation.gov.uk revised text) — cross-checked against gov.uk/pension-credit/eligibility
SPC Regulations 2002 Sch 6 paras 3, 4A, 5 (legislation.gov.uk revised text)
SPC Act 2002 s.4(1A) (legislation.gov.uk revised text); gov.uk/pension-credit/eligibility (Housing Benefit saving)
Pensions Act 1995 Sch 4 para 1 as amended (legislation.gov.uk revised text)
